# Tableau Public Dashboard — Build Guide

This folder holds the data extracts (produced by the notebook's "Tableau Exports" section) and the build instructions for a Tableau Public dashboard that visualizes the CDI analysis and the trained Random Forest's predictions.

## 0. Inputs (re-generated by the notebook)

Running `CSE469FinalProject.ipynb` end-to-end writes four CSVs here:

| File | Rows | Used by |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cdi_observations.csv`     | ~cleaned rowcount | map, demographics, topic mix, outlier table |
| `cdi_state_topic_year.csv` | (state x topic x year) | trend line |
| `cdi_predictions.csv`      | held-out test size | risk map, confusion matrix |
| `model_metrics.csv`        | 1 | scorecard |

## 1. Tooling

- **Tableau Public account** — free, at <https://public.tableau.com/>
- **Tableau Desktop Public Edition** (Mac/Windows) — free download from the same site
- **Linux users:** use **Web Authoring** in a browser on `public.tableau.com` (Create > Web Authoring). The build steps below are identical from "Connect Data" onward.

## 2. Data sources

In a new workbook:

1. Connect to `cdi_observations.csv` — name the data source `Observations`.
2. Add `cdi_state_topic_year.csv` — name `StateTopicYear`.
3. Add `cdi_predictions.csv` — name `Predictions`.
4. Add `model_metrics.csv` — name `Metrics`.
5. Create relationships on `LocationAbbr`, `Topic`, `YearStart` where shared.
6. **Convert to Tableau Hyper Extract** (right-click data source > "Extract Data..."). Tableau Public publishes the extract, not a live connection.

## 3. Worksheets

Build one sheet per row. Drag fields to the listed shelves.

| # | Sheet              | Data source     | Marks  | Columns / Rows                                              | Color / Size                | Filters                  |
| - | ------------------ | --------------- | ------ | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- | ------------------------ |
| 1 | `map_burden`       | Observations    | Map    | LocationAbbr -> Geographic role: State                      | AVG(DataValue)              | Topic, YearStart         |
| 2 | `trend_by_topic`   | StateTopicYear  | Line   | YearStart -> Columns; mean_data_value -> Rows               | Topic                       | LocationAbbr, Topic      |
| 3 | `demographics`     | Observations    | Bar    | CNT(rows) -> Columns; Stratification1 -> Rows               | Stratification1             | Topic, YearStart         |
| 4 | `topic_mix`        | Observations    | Square | Topic -> Detail                                             | Topic; Size=CNT(rows)       | LocationAbbr, YearStart  |
| 5 | `risk_map`         | Predictions     | Map    | LocationAbbr -> Geographic role: State                      | MODE(predicted_RiskLevel)   | YearStart                |
| 6 | `confusion_matrix` | Predictions     | Square | predicted_RiskLevel -> Columns; true_RiskLevel -> Rows      | CNT(rows); Label: CNT(rows) | (none)                   |
| 7 | `model_scorecard`  | Metrics         | Text   | Measure Names -> Rows                                       | (none)                      | (none)                   |
| 8 | `outlier_table`    | Observations    | Text   | LocationAbbr, Topic, YearStart, DataValue -> Rows           | (none)                      | is_outlier_iqr = TRUE OR is_outlier_iso = TRUE; Topic |

Tip for sheet 5: create a calculated field `RiskLevelOrder` mapping {Low: 1, Moderate: 2, High: 3, Very High: 4}, then color the map by that integer for a meaningful red-yellow-green ramp.

## 4. Dashboards

### Dashboard A — "Chronic Disease Landscape" (non-technical audience)
- Size: 1366 x 900, fixed.
- Layout:
  - **Title row:** "Chronic Disease Burden in the U.S., 2001–2021"
  - **Filters row** (apply to all sheets on this dashboard): YearStart range slider, Topic multi-select, LocationAbbr multi-select
  - **Row 1:** `map_burden` (left, 55% width) + `trend_by_topic` (right, 45%)
  - **Row 2:** `demographics` (left, 50%) + `topic_mix` (right, 50%)

### Dashboard B — "Risk Prediction Model" (technical audience)
- Size: 1366 x 900, fixed.
- Layout:
  - **Title row:** "Random Forest Risk-Level Classifier"
  - **Row 1:** `model_scorecard` (left, 35%) + `confusion_matrix` (right, 65%)
  - **Row 2:** `risk_map` (full width)
  - **Row 3:** `outlier_table` (full width)

### Optional Story
Sequence: Problem (text) -> Dashboard A -> Dashboard B -> Recommendations (text, four bullets from README §7).

## 5. Publish

1. `Server > Tableau Public > Save to Tableau Public As...`
2. Workbook name: `chronic-disease-risks-us`
3. After save, open the URL Tableau hands back — format:
   `https://public.tableau.com/app/profile/<your-handle>/viz/chronic-disease-risks-us/DashboardA`
4. Workbook settings -> enable **"Show sheets as tabs"** so the second dashboard is reachable.

## 6. Wire the URL into the repo

1. In the project root `README.md`, replace `<TABLEAU_PUBLIC_URL_TBD>` in the "Interactive Dashboard" section with the URL from step 5.
2. Take a screenshot of Dashboard A, save it as `asset/tableau_thumbnail.png`, and the README's image embed will resolve.
3. Commit on `claude/review-ds-project-nGPIJ`.

## 7. Sanity checks before sharing the link

- Open the published URL in an incognito window — both dashboards load, no errors.
- Flip the Topic filter on Dashboard A — colors on `map_burden` change.
- Spot-check one cell of `confusion_matrix` against the `classification_report` printed by the notebook — counts must match.
- `model_scorecard` numbers match the values printed by the GridSearchCV / evaluation cells.
